title: Understand: The Battle for Voting Rights
description: Check your understanding of the battle for voting rights both inside and outside of the court system with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Elections and Voting Rights: From the Founding through the Civil Rights Movement
Understand: The Battle for Voting Rights

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these was a key method the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) used to fight for racial equality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Bringing legal challenges through courts, including the Supreme Court
Organizing relocation from southern states to northern states
Patrolling Black neighborhoods to protect people from racial violence
Encouraging voters to vote without paying poll taxes

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the purpose of the Southern Christian Leadership Conference (SCLC)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To protest racial inequality through nonviolent protests
To achieve the end of slavery in the United States
To secure voting rights for Black people
To foster the concept of Black Power

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which groups played a significant role in organizing student-led sit-in movement? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which organizations were responsible for sponsoring Freedom Rides as a way to enforce the Supreme Court’s ruling in *Boynton v. Virginia*?  **

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which Supreme Court cases banned segregation in public places?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: *Heart of Atlanta Motel, Inc. v. United States*

assessment_items.question_content:
**Prior to becoming the first Black Supreme Court Justice, Thurgood Marshall served as counsel for which organization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What was the purpose of the Montgomery Bus Boycott?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To protest segregation in the public bus system
To enforce the Supreme Court’s ruling in *Boynton v. Virginia*
To advocate for equal pay for White and Black bus drivers
To fight for public school buses for all-Black schools

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these actions were forms of protest used by Black civil right activists in the 1950s and 1960s? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What was the goal of the Twenty-Fourth Amendment? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To eliminate the use of poll taxes in federal elections
To formally grant voting rights to Black men
To lower the voting age from twenty-one to eighteen
To give Washington, D.C. electoral votes

assessment_items.question_content:
The Supreme Court held in *Breedlove v. Suttles* (1937) that poll taxes in state elections did not violate the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment. 

**Which of the following reversed this ruling?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these Supreme Court cases held that poll taxes in all state and local elections violated the Equal Protection Clause?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these common barriers to voting in the 1960s was not addressed by the ratification of the Twenty-Fourth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
